danger_signals.visual_form: Complete formations of enemy ships advancing on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Threat requiring evasion or destruction; destroying complete formations yields power-ups

danger_signals.visual_form: Large distinctive enemy entity at stage end
danger_signals.meaning: Stage culmination threat; defeat required to progress or reach alternate ending
Environmental hazard with opportunity; flashing meteors contain power-ups
Destructible enemy formations with power-up rewards

safety_signals.visual_form: Red bubbles (shot/bomb power), blue bubbles (shield/level), green bubbles (bonus/ship)
safety_signals.meaning: Player capability enhancement; defensive and offensive strengthening

safety_signals.visual_form: Stage checkpoints (unmarked in-game, implied by respawn mechanics)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression markers; ship destruction returns player to last checkpoint rather than stage start

safety_signals.visual_form: Unique power object distinct from standard bubbles
safety_signals.meaning: Exceptional temporary capability: all-enemy-clear or powerful laser beam

faction_indicators.color_identity: Green (original Darius), Blue (Darius II variant), Red (new prototype)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Silver Hawk craft design (three distinct visual variants)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player ship color indicates which continuity/variant is active

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types (ships, amoebas, meteors, boss entities)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Opposition to human/alliance forces implied by shmup paradigm

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Horizontal scrolling environment with enemy formations and obstacles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies advance; player navigates and destroys; stage scrolls forward

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large boss entity appears at stage culmination
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard enemies cease; boss attacks; defeat triggers stage completion or ending
Destructible meteors appear; flashing variants contain power-ups; environmental hazard with reward opportunity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: No power-up bar displayed; player shot/bomb intensity changes with power-up acquisition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shot and bomb power reset to minimum if ship destroyed; power-ups restore capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player respawns at checkpoint with reduced power
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Return to last checkpoint; loss of all accumulated power-ups; progression continues from safe point
Player selects forward or upward path; different paths lead to different boss encounters and endings

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of three final stages (L, N, O) contains its own boss and leads to distinct ending, suggesting multiple narrative outcomes based on player choice
15-stage map with forward/upward path choices creates branching progression; player agency in destination selection implies different mission objectives or theaters
Three acquisition methods (enemy formation destruction, meteor destruction, cage destruction) suggest multiple enemy supply lines and environmental hazards coexist
Three playable Silver Hawk variants reference previous Darius games, suggesting this is a continuation or cross-over event within larger conflict

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Shoots projectiles (Y button default), drops bombs (B button default), switches to side lasers (R button); maintains position unless destroyed
Resets to last stage checkpoint; all shot and bomb upgrades lost; capability returns to minimum level
Advances across screen; must be completely destroyed to yield power-up reward
Descend across screen; flashing variants contain power-ups; destroyable; present hazard and opportunity simultaneously
Unique attack pattern; defeat required for progression; each of three final bosses (L/N/O stages) leads to distinct ending
Static; destructible; releases power-up upon destruction
If destroyed by player fire: clears all enemies on screen; if grabbed by player: grants powerful laser beam; temporary effect

notes: Darius Force operates as a shmup without explicit faction narrative or political context in available sources. World-state communication relies entirely on visual/behavioral mechanics rather than story exposition. The branching 15-stage map and three distinct endings suggest narrative agency, but specific story/political content is not documented. The three Silver Hawk variants (green/blue/red) serve as mechanical/visual distinction rather than faction markers. Power-up system and checkpoint mechanics are core to world-state visualization—destruction resets capability, reinforcing progression stakes. Stage-end boss encounters and path branching create implicit narrative diversity without explicit exposition.
